title: Gap Fill: The Story of the Internet

tasks.instruction: Przeczytaj ponizszy tekst, z ktorego usunieto szesc zdan. Dobierz zdania oznaczone literami (A-H) tak, aby tekst byl spojny i logiczny. Dwa zdania zostaly podane dodatkowo i nie pasuja do zadnego fragmentu.
tasks.text:
THE STORY OF THE INTERNET

The internet is so deeply woven into modern life that it is easy to forget how recently it was invented. The technology that now connects over five billion people began as a modest experiment linking just four computers at American universities. _________ (1.1) The speed at which it evolved from a specialist tool into a global necessity is one of the most remarkable transformations in the history of technology.

The story begins in the late 1960s, during the Cold War. The US Department of Defense funded a project called ARPANET, whose central innovation was a method of breaking data into small packets and routing them independently across a network. _________ (1.2) On 29 October 1969, the first message was sent between UCLA and Stanford Research Institute. The system crashed after transmitting just two letters, yet the underlying concept had been validated.

For over twenty years, the network remained the preserve of academics and military researchers. _________ (1.3) His system of web pages, hyperlinks, and URLs transformed the internet from a text-based tool for specialists into a visual, navigable space accessible to anyone with a computer and a phone line.

The mid-1990s saw an explosion of commercial activity online. Entrepreneurs rushed to create internet businesses, and investors poured billions into start-ups, many of which had little more than an idea and a website. _________ (1.4) When the bubble burst in 2000, trillions of dollars in market value evaporated, yet the companies that survived, such as Amazon and eBay, went on to reshape global commerce.

The arrival of smartphones in the late 2000s triggered the internet's second great transformation. _________ (1.5) In many developing countries, the mobile phone became the primary means of accessing the internet, leapfrogging the desktop computer entirely.

Today, the internet underpins virtually every aspect of modern society, from banking and healthcare to entertainment and politics. _________ (1.6) Questions of privacy, misinformation, and digital inequality are now as pressing as the technical challenges that the internet's pioneers once faced.

options.text: That changed in 1991, when British scientist Tim Berners-Lee, working at the CERN laboratory in Switzerland, invented the World Wide Web.
This approach meant that even if large sections of the network were destroyed, data could still find alternative routes to its destination.
This period of frenzied speculation became known as the dot-com bubble, and it was driven more by optimism than by sound business planning.
Suddenly, people could go online from anywhere at any time, fundamentally changing how they communicated, consumed media, and conducted business.
Yet this extraordinary power has also created challenges that its creators never anticipated.
Its journey from those four connected machines to a network spanning the entire planet took less than fifty years.
Early internet users communicated primarily through email and text-based discussion forums known as Usenet groups.
Governments in several countries have attempted to restrict or censor internet access in order to control the flow of information.
